changes_summary:
**Changes from v1:**

**Step 7 (flag-actiondrag-coordinator-scope):** (1) Removed `ref` from the public `ActionDragCoordinator` API — the raw ref is fully internal to the hook, only accessible via method calls. (2) Expanded migration scope from 2 files to the full 5-file threading chain: `useTimelineData.ts`, `useTimelineState.ts`, `TimelineEditor.tsx`, `useClipDrag.ts`, and `useLibraryCallbacks.ts` (plus `useTimelineEditing.ts` orchestrator). (3) Added `getInitial()` method. (4) Added validation step: grep for `actionDragStateRef` — zero matches remaining. (5) Added guard in `clear()` against stale updates after clearing.

**Step 8 (flag-clipaction-pointerdown-assumption):** Reversed approach entirely — all three `stopPropagation` calls are **kept**, not removed. Added detailed analysis of the event flow proving `onPointerDown` stopPropagation is load-bearing: pointer capture only happens after a move threshold, so bubbled pointerdown would reach the timeline library's drag init during the threshold window. Step 8 is now documentation-only (adding comments). Updated success criteria accordingly.

**Success criteria updated:** (1) ClipAction criterion changed from "exactly 2 stopPropagation" to "retains all 3 with explaining comments". (2) ActionDragState criterion adds "no raw ref exposed in coordinator API" and "migration covers all 5 files". (3) New criterion: grep for `actionDragStateRef` returns zero matches. (4) Removed stale assumption about capture-phase handling pointerdown bubbling.
questions: Step 9 (height hack): The current hack uses Math.max(200, ...) as a floor. Should the minimum editor pane height be the same MIN_EDITOR_HEIGHT (200) already defined in PanesContext, or does the video editor need a different minimum?
success_criteria: useTimelineEditing.ts reduced from ~947 lines to ~250 lines, with 4–5 focused sub-hooks
Zero inline styles on the drop indicator root element (moved to CSS class); dynamic position styles remain inline
Library callback return-value contracts documented with JSDoc on each callback
ActionDragState mutations go through a coordinator API — no direct ref.current[id] = ... outside the coordinator, and no raw ref exposed in the coordinator's public API

ActionDragState coordinator migration covers all files in the threading chain: useTimelineData, useTimelineState, TimelineEditor.tsx, useClipDrag, useLibraryCallbacks, and the useTimelineEditing orchestrator
Grep for actionDragStateRef in src/tools/video-editor/ returns zero matches after Step 7
ClipAction retains all 3 stopPropagation calls (onPointerDown, onClick, onDoubleClick), each with a comment explaining the event-flow dependency
EditorPaneTab has no local height subtraction hack — effectiveEditorPaneHeight from PanesContext is used directly
getEffectivePaneHeights handles generations-pane visibility, with updated tests
npm run build passes after every step
Manual smoke test: create/move/resize/drag-drop/delete/split clips all work
assumptions: The timeline library (@xzdarcy/react-timeline-editor) callback return-value semantics (false=reject, undefined=accept) are stable and won't change

The timeline library registers its own pointerdown handler on bubble phase (not capture), so ClipAction's stopPropagation on onPointerDown effectively prevents the library from starting a competing drag during the threshold window before useClipDrag takes pointer capture
MIN_EDITOR_HEIGHT (200px) in PanesContext is the correct floor for the video editor as well — no separate minimum needed
The existing CSS classes in timeline-overrides.css (.drop-indicator-row, .drop-indicator-line, etc.) are already styled correctly; only the root container is missing a CSS class
No other consumers of getEffectivePaneHeights exist outside PanesProvider — extending its signature is safe
